title: xerochrysum viscosum
intro: Xerochrysum viscosum, also known as sticky everlasting, is a flowering plant native to Australia, characterized by its yellow flowers and sticky, glandular leaves. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Aboriginal medicine, where it has been used to treat various ailments, including wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Xerochrysum viscosum include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which have been shown to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal remedy.
